content.full_text:
~~alifornia 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Ebbie Mouton 
Deputy City Clerk 
P. O. Box 1007 
Cudahy, CA 
90201 
Dear Ms. Mouton: 
September 13, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-346 
You have requested assistance with regard to your review of 
statements filed by four members of the Cudahy city Council and 
their jointly controlled committee, citizens for Progress of the 
city of Cudahy, under the campaign disclosure provisions of the 
Political Reform Act.li 
QUESTION 
Have the councilmembers and the jointly controlled committee 
properly filed the campaign disclosure statements required under 
the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
A review of the disclosure statements indicates that some 
amendments are needed. 
These are discussed in the analysis 
below. 
Please note that this review did not include any 
investigation into the accuracy of the information disclosed on 
the statements, but focused on whether proper forms were filed and 
whether certain information was completely disclosed. 
FACTS 
You have provided copies of campaign disclosure statements 
filed for 1987 and the first six months of 1988 for councilmembers 
Wilfred Colon, Joseph Graffio, John O. Robertson, Jr., and John 
Thomas Thurman, and their jointly controlled committee, citizens 
for Progress of the City of Cudahy. 
You also have provided copies 
liGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Ebbie Mouton 
Page Two 
of the committee's statement of organization (Form 410) filed 
February 11, 1986, and two amended statements of organization 
filed March 18, 1986 and April 13, 1987. 
Initially, and on the amendment filed March 18, 1986, the 
statement of organization disclosed that the committee was 
controlled by Councilmembers Graffio, Colon and Thurman. 
On 
April 13, 1987, an amendment was filed which stated that the 
committee was no longer controlled and was a general purpose 
committee. 
On all three statements of organization, Mr. Colon was 
listed as committee treasurer and Mr. Graffio was listed as 
committee secretary. 
Throughout the 16-month period January 1, 1987 - June 30, 
1988, all three councilmembers, as well as Mr. Robertson, filed 
individual campaign statements (Form 490) indicating that they 
controlled the committee. 
On its first semi-annual campaign 
statement for 1987, the committee reported that it was controlled 
by all four councilmembers. 
On its second semi-annual statement, 
it reported that only Mr. Robertson controlled the committee, 
although Mr. Colon signed the statement as treasurer. 
For the 
period January 1, 1988 through June 30, 1988, the committee's 
campaign statement again listed all four councilmembers as 
controlling. 
On the individual semi-annual campaign disclosure statements 
filed by the four councilmembers covering January 1, 1987 through 
December 31, 1987, each also indicated that he was filing a 
consolidated report with the committee. 
However, the committee's 
activities were not included on the councilmembers' campaign 
statements, and the committee actually filed separate semi-annual 
statements for 1987. 
For the first six months of 1988, Councilmembers Colon, 
Graffio and Thurman again filed individual campaign statements 
which indicated that they were filing consolidated statements 
with the committee when in fact they were not. 
These statements 
were amended to clarify that they controlled the committee, but 
that they had not included the committee's activities on their 
statements. 
Also during the first six months of 1988, it appears that Mr. 
Robertson was being voted upon in the city's April 12 election. 
He filed two pre-election campaign statements which included the 
committee's activities. 
On July 28, 1988, Mr. Robertson amended 
the pre-election statements to exclude the committee's activities, 
although he acknowledged that he controlled the committee. 
On his 
semi-annual statement covering March 26, 1988 through June 30, 
1988, he indicated that he controlled the committee but was not 
including the committee's activities in the statement. 


Ebbie Mouton 
Page Three 
The committee filed a separate campaign statement for the 
period January 1, 1988 through June 30, 1988. 
However, it does 
not appear to include all of the activities which occurred during 
the six-month period. 
The committee again states that it was 
controlled by all four councilmembers. 
ANALYSIS 
Amendments to the Statement of Organization 
The Act requires officeholders, candidates and their 
controlled committees to file periodic campaign disclosure 
reports. 
(Section 84200 et seq.) 
Section 82016 states: 
"Controlled committee" means a committee which is controlled 
directly or indirectly by a candidate or state measure 
proponent or which acts jointly with a candidate, controlled 
committee or state measure proponent in connection with the 
making of expenditures. 
A candidate or state measure 
proponent controls a committee if he, his agent or any other 
committee he controls has a significant influence on the 
actions or decisions of the committee. 
From the facts outlined above, it appears that all four 
officeholders have controlled the committee for the entire 
16-month period January I, 1987 through June 30, 1988. 
Therefore, 
amendments are needed to the statement of organization. 
First, 
the amendment filed on April 13, 1987, which stated that the 
committee was not controlled, should be withdrawn. 
In addition, 
the statement must be amended to indicate when Mr. Robertson began 
his involvement with the committee. 
The simplest method of bringing the statement of organization 
into compliance would probably be for the committee to complete a 
new amended Form 410 providing its current status. 
The amended 
Form 410 should be filed with a cover letter explaining the 
amendments which should have been filed during 1987 and 1988. 
Any 
councilmember who has a significant influence on the actions or 
decisions of the committee, must be listed as controlling the 
committee. 
You should also inform the committee of the requirement in 
section 84103 that when information contained in a statement of 
organization changes, an amendment must be filed within 10 days to 
reflect the change. 
In addition, if any of the following 
information changes during the 16 days prior to a city election, 
the amended information must be filed with you by personal 
delivery or telegram within 24 hours: 
1. 
The name of the committee. 


Ebbie Mouton 
Page Four 
2. 
The name of the treasurer or other principal 
officers. 
3. 
The name of any candidate or committee by which the 
committee is controlled or with which it acts 
jointly. 
section 84103(b). 
Amendments to the Committee's Campaign statements 
With regard to the committee's campaign disclosure statements, 
the following items need to be amended: 
1. 
The committee's 1987 statements cover the periods 
January I-July 31 and July 31-December 31. 
The correct periods 
covered are January I-June 30 and July I-December 31. 
Activity 
which occurred after June 30, 1987, should be removed from the 
first semi-annual statement and disclosed on the second 
semi-annual statement. 
2. 
On the committee's statement covering January I-July 31, 
1987, Schedule G shows a $77 increase to cash from Mr. Thurman. 
The same payment appears to have been reported as a monetary 
contribution from Mr. Thurman on Schedule A. 
The $77 increase 
disclosed on Schedule G was not included in the calculations for 
the statement of changes in financial condition (lines 13-19) on 
the Summary Page. 
Please also note that these two payments are 
listed as having been received on July 27, which is after the 
correct closing date for this statement. 
3. 
On the committee's statement covering July 31-
December 31, 1987, the following errors have been noted: 
a. 
Only Mr. Robertson has been listed on the cover page 
as controlling the committee. 
b. 
The Summary Page does not contain cumulative figures 
from January 1 (Columns A and C). 
c. 
Although the cover page indicates that the statement 
covers through December 31, 1987, all subsequent pages 
indicate that the statement covers through January 31, 1988. 
4. 
The committee's statement covering January I-June 30, 
1988, appears to cover only the period March 26-June 30. 
Because 
the committee erroneously filed two consolidated pre-election 
statements with Mr. Robertson in connection with the city's 
April 12, 1988, election and Mr. Robertson has amended his filings 
to exclude the committee's activities, the committee must now file 
two pre-election statements and a semi-annual statement for the 
first six months of 1988. 
In addition, the closing date used on 


Ebbie Mouton 
Page Five 
the second pre-election statement is incorrect. 
The correct 
periods covered in connection with the April 12, 1988 election are: 
January 1 - February 27 
February 27 - March 26 
March 27-June 30 
Amendments to the Councilmembers' statements 
With regard to the councilmembers' statements, the following 
items should be amended: 
1. 
Each councilmember must amend his semi-annual statements 
filed for 1987 to indicate that he was not filing a consolidated 
statement with the committee. 
The committee should be listed in 
Part III of the cover page to the Form 490, rather than in Part II. 
2. 
As with the committee, there appears to be some confusion 
about the periods covered for the semi-annual statements filed for 
1987. 
For example, several of the cover pages show January I-June 
30, but the attached schedules show January I-July 31. 
The 
correct periods covered are January I-June 30 and July 1-
December 31, 1987, and the information disclosed on the attached 
schedules must reflect activity for the correct periods. 
3. 
Most of the individual statements filed by the 
councilmembers also must be amended because they do not contain 
completed Summary Pages, nor do they indicate the source(s) of the 
funds used to make the expenditures each has disclosed. 
When a 
candidate or officeholder uses personal funds to make expenditures 
and does not anticipate being repaid, the funds must be disclosed 
on Schedule A as monetary contributions from the candidate or 
officeholder. 
For further information on how to report a 
candidate's personal funds, refer to pages 112 and 113 of the 1988 
Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the 
Political Reform Act. 
Although I have not performed a detailed review of all of the 
information disclosed on each of the statements you provided, I 
did notice that some information was incomplete with regard to 
addresses, dates of contributions, and employer names. 
You may 
wish to review the statements yourself to determine what other 
amendments may be needed. 
In addition, please note that when a committee is controlled 
by more than one candidate or officeholder, each candidate or 
officeholder must file a separate campaign statement (Form 490) 
and the jointly controlled committee must file separately on Form 
420. 
An individual candidate or officeholder may not file a 
consolidated campaign statement with a committee which is also 
controlled by another candidate or officeholder. 


Ebbie Mouton 
Page Six 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you 
have any questions. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
{! ~ t ;2-.<,,-
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 


August 9, 1988 
Ms. Carla Wardlow 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804-0807 
C~ 
Dear Ms .~lbW1 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of July 28, 1988, I 
am 
enclosing copies of the Statements listed below for your review, 
which have been filed with the City Clerk's office. 
1. 
Statements covering period 1/1/87 through 6/30/87. 
Form 
490 
for 
Wilfred 
Colon, 
Joseph 
Graffio, 
John 
O. 
Robertson, 
Jr., 
and 
John 
Thomas 
Thurman, 
and 
Form 
420 
for Citizens for Progress of the City of Cudahy. 
2. 
Statements covering period 7/1/87 through 12/31/87. 
Form 
490 
for 
Wilfred 
Colon, 
Joseph 
Graffio, 
John 
O. 
Robertson, 
and 
John 
Thomas 
Thurman, 
and 
Form 
420 
for 
Citizens for Progress of the City of Cudahy. 
3. 
Statements covering period 1/1/88 through 6/30/88. 
Forms 490 and 405 for Wilfred Colon, John Thomas Thurman, 
and Joseph Graffio, and Form 420 for Citizens for progress 
of the City of Cudahy. 
4. 
Statements covering period 1/1/88 through 2/26/88. 
Forms 490 and 405 for John O. 
Robertson. 
No filing for 
Citizens for Progress of the City of Cudahy. 
5. 
Statements covering period 2/27/88 through 3/25/88. 
Forms 490 and 405 for John O. 
Robertson. 
No filing for 
Citizens for Progress of the City of Cudahy. 
6. 
88. 


Carla Wardlow 
FPPC 
-2-
t 9,1988 
(Please note that I have not included copies of Statements filed 
by Council Member/Candidate Gabriel Zippi and Candidates Faye 
Dunlap and Joseph Fregeau, there being no problem with their 
Statements.) 
As you will recall, several questions developed during our con-
versations regarding the Statements filed by the Council Members 
and Statements of Organization filed by the Committee with the 
Secretary of State when Council Member/Candidate John O. Robertson 
requested to file amendments 
to his Campaign Statements Form 
490 and file separately from the Committee. 
You stated on the 
telephone that after you have had a chance to review the above 
Statements, 
you 
would 
write 
a 
letter outlining 
step-by-step 
any amendments that should be made. 
Thank you for all of your help in the past and if you have any 
questions or need any further information, please call me at 
(213) 773-5143. 
Enclosures 
cc: 
Gerald M. Caton, City Clerk 
e/l/068 
Ebbie Mouton 
Deputy City Clerk
